question: Identify the root cause of the network fault: which device, what changed, and why it breaks.
description:
R2 lowers MTU on GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0 (R2↔R3 primary path) from 1514
to 1400. R3 stays at default. IS-IS CSNP/PSNP packets sized for the
larger LSP-MTU can no longer be reassembled across the link; IS-IS
adjacency stalls in INIT and never reaches UP. The lab fails over to
the backup path R2→R6→R7→R4 (metric 60). Classic "IGP stuck with link
up and pings working" production fault.
expected_rca:
Root cause: on 100.64.0.102 (R2), the MTU on GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0 (the R2<->R3 primary path) was lowered to 1400 while R3 stays at 1514.
The asymmetric MTU means the oversize LSP/CSNP exchange cannot complete across the link, so the IS-IS link-state databases cannot synchronize and the adjacency cannot reach full operation. As a result the primary path is unusable for IS-IS and traffic to R4 routes over the backup R2->R6->R7->R4 path (metric 60).
Fix: remove the MTU override on R2's GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0 (restore 1514).
expected_symptoms_summary:
No fault syslog observed after apply — silent scenario; small IS-IS
hello PDUs may still cross the asymmetric MTU and keep the adjacency
Up, while large LSP/CSNP exchange fails. Diagnostic ground truth
lives in expected_state_diffs.
expected_state_diffs: R2 `show interfaces GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0`: MTU 1400 (baseline: 1514). R3's Gi0/0/0/0: still default — asymmetric L3 MTU on the R2-R3 primary.
R2 `show running-config interface GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0`: `mtu 1400` line present (baseline: line absent).
Any router `ping <R3-loopback> source Loopback0 size 1500`: fragmentation-needed / drops; `size 1000`: succeeds — asymmetric MTU breaks large-packet traversal.
R2 `show isis interface GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0`: per-interface state may flag MTU mismatch; full-size LSP exchange from R3 is dropped on R2.
R2 `show route 192.0.2.4` (R4 loopback): if the LSP exchange is impaired, the path may shift to the backup R6→R7 with higher metric (~60) instead of the primary R2-R3-R4 path.

revert.commands:
configure terminal
interface GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0
 no mtu
